text.title: Rust Error Handling Core Rules
text.summary: Testable rules for rust-error-handling. Each rule pairs binary check + sourced rationale. Last rule is the terminal skip-this-methodology guard.

rule.statement: Each service MUST define exactly one AppError enum (thiserror-derived) covering domain + infrastructure variants.
rule.rationale: Multiple ad-hoc error types fragment the error story and break consistent HTTP mapping.

rule.statement: Library crates MUST use thiserror for typed enums; never expose Box<dyn Error> across crate API boundaries.
rule.rationale: Typed enums are matchable; Box<dyn Error> erases information consumers need.

rule.statement: Binary main()/CLI glue layers MAY use anyhow::Result for ergonomic bubble-up but MUST translate into AppError before crossing service boundaries.
rule.rationale: anyhow is great for one-off scripts; pushing it into APIs breaks downstream pattern matching.

rule.statement: All fallible call sites use ? plus thiserror #[from] conversions; manual map_err only when context must be added.
rule.rationale: From conversions keep call sites clean; explicit map_err only when wrapping with context.

rule.statement: unwrap() and expect() forbidden outside tests and short-lived main glue; CI MUST deny clippy::unwrap_used + clippy::expect_used.
rule.rationale: Panics in production cause request drops, often correlated with the worst user-facing edge cases.

rule.statement: If the preconditions in `Applies If` fail or any of the `Skip If` conditions is true, return `skipped` and apply no further rules.
rule.rationale: Methodologies are not universal; running them outside their fit-window wastes effort and produces wrong outputs.
